title: guarea pterorhachis subsp. submarginalis
intro: Guarea pterorhachis subsp. submarginalis is a plant species within the family Meliaceae, commonly known as a tropical tree native to Central America and the Caribbean. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and seeds, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Guarea pterorhachis subsp. submarginalis include its potential use in treating fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts are also used in herbal preparations to support overall well-being.
